children.name: Chaetops
children.description: A genus of two medium-sized, ground-dwelling passerines endemic to the mountainous regions of southern Africa, known as rockjumpers for their habit of hopping and clambering over rocky outcrops and boulders. They have strong legs and feet, a long tail that is frequently cocked, and a slightly decurved bill. Their plumage combines warm rufous, grey, and white in a distinctive pattern.

children.name: Chaetops frenatus
children.description: A striking, rufous-and-grey bird with a bold white eyebrow and black-and-white striped, fan-shaped tail, found on rocky mountain slopes of the Cape region of South Africa. It is a highly specialised rock dweller, hopping with agility over boulders and scree slopes in fynbos heathlands. Its ringing, whistled call echoes across the rocky valleys of its limited range.

children.name: Chaetops aurantius
children.description: A colourful rockjumper with a bright orange belly, a white throat, and a long black tail tipped with white, found in the high-altitude grasslands and rocky slopes of the Drakensberg Mountains of Lesotho and South Africa. It lives in small family groups that forage for insects on rocky terrain, often in association with other highland birds. Its display involves a bounding, fluttering flight accompanied by loud, melodious calls.
